_note: A 4-turn headless customer-support agent loop. Stable system+tools prefix (cacheable), growing history, and a verbose volatile tool output each turn that carries one decision-driving line buried in noise. DECISION: markers are what the offline DeterministicRunner keys on, so ablation/certification have ground truth.

blocks.text:
You are an autonomous customer-support agent handling refund and order-dispute tickets headless, without a human in the loop.
You look up orders, consult the refund policy knowledge base, issue refunds, and close tickets strictly within the limits set below.
Never issue a refund for an amount that exceeds the order total, and never waive restocking fees without explicit policy authorization.
DECISION: auto-refund orders under $200 within 30 days; escalate otherwise

AVAILABLE TOOLS (json schema, abbreviated):
- lookup_order(order_id): returns order status, line items, amount, purchase date, and customer info as JSON.
- kb_search(query): searches the internal refund-policy knowledge base; returns top-3 matching policy paragraphs.
- issue_refund(order_id, amount, method): posts a refund to the original payment method or store credit; returns confirmation JSON.
- escalate(ticket_id, reason, tier): routes the ticket to a human agent at the specified tier (L2 or L3).
- annotate_ticket(ticket_id, note, status): appends an audit note and sets the ticket status (open|resolved|escalated).
DECISION: tool issue_refund is available

lookup_order("ORD-884712") -> {"order_id":"ORD-884712","customer_id":"cust-30291","customer_email":"jane.doe@example.com","status":"delivered","total_usd":89.00,"currency":"USD","items":[{"sku":"WIDGET-BLU-M","qty":1,"unit_price":89.00}],"purchased_at":"2026-06-09T14:22:00Z","delivered_at":"2026-06-12T10:05:00Z","payment_method":"visa_ending_4821"}
fetched order in 38ms; cache=miss; tenant=acme-store; region=us-east-1
info: order age is 12 days from purchase date 2026-06-09 to today 2026-06-21
info: order total $89.00 is below the $200 auto-refund threshold
info: payment method on file: visa_ending_4821 (original method, eligible for reversal)
DECISION: order eligible for auto-refund, $89.00 within 12 days, no manager approval needed
info: customer has no prior refund requests in the last 180 days
info: item WIDGET-BLU-M is non-perishable; restocking policy applies if item not returned
debug: query_hash=a3f1 evaluated_at=2026-06-21T10:02:14Z tenant=acme-store
debug: order index freshness: 0s (real-time lookup, no cache)

RETRIEVED (speculative context, similarity=0.69): 'Seasonal Promotion Terms and Conditions — Summer Sale 2026' — This document covers promotional discount stacking rules, exclusion lists for sale items, and gift-card redemption limits during the summer sale window running June 1 through August 31. It was pulled by a broad semantic retrieval on 'order refund customer' and is not cited anywhere in the resolution path for this ticket. Appendix B lists all excluded SKUs; Appendix C covers influencer promo codes and their non-refundable status under specific campaign terms. None of these terms apply to the current order.
Ticket TKT-20941: Customer jane.doe@example.com requests a refund for order ORD-884712. Process the request per policy.

You are an autonomous customer-support agent handling refund and order-dispute tickets headless, without a human in the loop.

DECISION: auto-refund orders under $200 within 30 days; escalate otherwise

AVAILABLE TOOLS (json schema, abbreviated):
- lookup_order(order_id): returns order status, line items, amount, purchase date, and customer info as JSON.
- kb_search(query): searches the internal refund-policy knowledge base; returns top-3 matching policy paragraphs.
- issue_refund(order_id, amount, method): posts a refund to the original payment method or store credit; returns confirmation JSON.
- escalate(ticket_id, reason, tier): routes the ticket to a human agent at the specified tier (L2 or L3).
- annotate_ticket(ticket_id, note, status): appends an audit note and sets the ticket status (open|resolved|escalated).
DECISION: tool issue_refund is available
[turn 0 summary] Looked up order ORD-884712; $89.00, delivered 2026-06-12, 12 days old, below $200 threshold. Customer jane.doe@example.com. Next: confirm refund policy via kb_search.

kb_search("refund policy standard order under $200 within 30 days") -> {"query":"refund policy standard order under $200 within 30 days","hits":[{"id":"pol-refund-001","score":0.94,"title":"Standard Refund Policy"},{"id":"pol-refund-002","score":0.81,"title":"Restocking Fee Schedule"},{"id":"pol-refund-003","score":0.76,"title":"Escalation Thresholds"}]}
pol-refund-001 (score 0.94): Orders under $200.00 purchased within the last 30 days are eligible for a full refund to the original payment method, no manager approval required.
pol-refund-001: Refund processing time is 3-5 business days for credit card reversals; 1 business day for store credit.
pol-refund-002 (score 0.81): A 15% restocking fee applies to non-perishable items returned in original packaging; fee is waived if item is defective or if customer was not at fault.
DECISION: refund permitted under pol-refund-001, no manager approval needed, full $89.00 to original method
pol-refund-002: Restocking fee waiver requires documented defect report or carrier damage claim; no waiver for change-of-mind returns without photo evidence.
pol-refund-003 (score 0.76): Orders above $200.00 or outside the 30-day window must be escalated to L2 for manual review before any credit is issued.
info: policy version v4.2 effective 2026-01-15; next review 2026-07-01
info: kb_search latency 61ms; index freshness 4h; tenant=acme-store
debug: embeddings model text-embed-v3, top_k=3, rerank=true

RETRIEVED (speculative context, similarity=0.65): 'Customer Loyalty Program — Tier Benefits and Point Accrual Rules' — A 14-section guide explaining how Bronze, Silver, and Gold tier customers accumulate and redeem loyalty points, including point multipliers during promotional windows and the blackout calendar for double-point events. Pulled because the customer record contains a loyalty tier field; entirely unrelated to the refund determination. The agent did not reference this document in the policy lookup.
Continue processing the refund for TKT-20941. Stay within policy.

issue_refund("ORD-884712", 89.00, "original") -> {"refund_id":"REF-554901","order_id":"ORD-884712","amount_usd":89.00,"method":"visa_ending_4821","status":"approved","initiated_at":"2026-06-21T10:04:37Z","expected_settlement":"2026-06-24T10:04:37Z","audit_id":"aud-ref-884712"}

info: refund of $89.00 posted to visa_ending_4821; settlement window 3 business days
info: customer notification email queued to jane.doe@example.com; template=refund_confirmation_v2
DECISION: refund of $89.00 issued to original payment method visa_ending_4821, no escalation
info: ledger entry created; refund_id REF-554901 linked to order ORD-884712 and ticket TKT-20941
info: no partial refund split; full order amount returned in one transaction
info: policy reference pol-refund-001 recorded in audit trail
debug: payment gateway latency 210ms; retry_count=0; tenant=acme-store; run=run-20941

RETRIEVED (speculative context, similarity=0.63): 'Payment Gateway Integration Guide — Retry Logic and Idempotency Keys' — An internal engineering document covering the retry backoff strategy for failed gateway calls, idempotency key construction, and how to reconcile partial settlements in the ledger. Surfaced because the refund call invoked the gateway; irrelevant to the business decision about whether the refund should be issued. The agent did not consult this document when processing the refund.
Confirm the refund was issued and close out ticket TKT-20941 per policy.
